qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public password-reset initiation leaks a live reset token in the API response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a reset-token disclosure in POST /api/customers/reset-password; the advisory is an unauthenticated SVG-processing DoS in GET /images, so the vulnerable endpoint, code path, and root cause do not align.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's GET /images SVG-processing denial-of-service root cause. The only qualifying finding is a customer password-reset token disclosure on a different public endpoint with a different vulnerable operation.
evidence: GHSA-m2q5-xhqg-92r2 describes unauthenticated denial of service via GET /images while processing SVG files with unbounded shadow-tree height or pattern-tile dimensions.

/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_fd742849/customer_reset_token_disclosure/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /api/customers/reset-password generating a reset token and returning the raw token in the JSON response through response.$body serialization.

The finding's reported code locations are under packages/evershop/src/modules/customer/api/resetPassword/* and the exploit path is reset-token issuance to response serialization, not /images or SVG parsing.

The session's only media-related issue is cms_upload_unbounded_memory_dos, but it is non_qualifying and concerns authenticated admin multipart upload buffering rather than the advisory's public GET /images SVG parser behavior.
